title: EduMap: AI-ML Powered Global Admission Navigator Website

requirements.text: Apply a cohesive visual theme across the entire application: use a clean academic-tech aesthetic with deep teal and amber accent colors, modern sans-serif typography, card-based layouts, and subtle gradient hero sections. The design should feel trustworthy, international, and accessible for student users.
acceptanceCriteria: All pages share consistent color palette (deep teal primary, amber accent)
Typography is readable and professional across all screen sizes
Navigation bar is present on all pages with the EduMap logo and key section links
Hero section on the landing page features a gradient background and a clear value proposition headline

requirements.text: Create a Landing / Home page that introduces EduMap with a hero section (headline, subheadline, call-to-action button), a feature highlights section showcasing the six core modules (Profile, Recommendations, Comparison, Maps, Guidelines, Chatbot), and a brief 'How It Works' step-by-step section.
acceptanceCriteria: Hero section renders with headline 'EduMap: AI-ML Powered Global Admission Navigator' and a prominent CTA button
Six feature cards are displayed with icons and short descriptions
'How It Works' section shows at least 3 steps (Enter Profile → Get Recommendations → Compare & Decide)
Page is responsive on mobile and desktop

requirements.text: Build a Student Profile form page where users can input their academic details: stream (Science/Commerce/Arts), percentage/marks, budget range (min/max in INR/USD), areas of interest (multi-select: Engineering, Medicine, Management, Law, Arts, etc.), preferred region (India / USA / UK / Canada / Australia / Other), and preferred college type (Government / Private / Deemed). Store submitted profiles in the backend.

quotes: Collects student data such as marks, stream, budget, interests, and preferred region

acceptanceCriteria: Form collects all specified fields with appropriate input types (dropdowns, sliders, checkboxes)
Form validates required fields before submission
On submission, profile data is saved to the backend canister
User receives a confirmation message after successful profile save
Saved profile can be retrieved and pre-filled on revisit

requirements.text: Implement a backend data model to store college records with the following fields: name, country, city, stream, courses offered, annual fees (INR/USD), ranking (national and global), eligibility criteria (minimum marks/scores), admission requirements (list of documents), application deadline, college type (Government/Private), and geographic coordinates (latitude, longitude).

quotes: centralizes essential data such as fees, rankings, eligibility criteria, admission requirements, and geographic information

acceptanceCriteria: College record type is defined in main.mo with all specified fields
Backend exposes functions to add, retrieve, and query college records
At least 20 seed college entries are pre-populated covering India and abroad (US, UK, Canada, Australia)
Query functions support filtering by stream, country, fee range, and ranking

requirements.text: Build a Recommendations page that takes the stored student profile, matches it against college records using a content-based scoring algorithm (weighing stream match, budget fit, marks eligibility, and region preference), and displays a ranked list of recommended colleges. Each result card must show college name, location, fees, ranking, match score, and a 'View Details' action.

quotes: Generates personalized college suggestions using machine learning algorithms
Content-Based Filtering Algorithm — Matches user profiles (marks, stream, budget) with college attributes

acceptanceCriteria: Recommendation engine runs on the backend and returns scored, sorted college list
Results page displays college cards sorted by match score (highest first)
Each card shows: name, city/country, annual fees, ranking, and a percentage match score
User can re-run recommendations after editing their profile
If no profile exists, user is redirected to the Profile form

requirements.text: Build a College Comparison page where users can select 2–4 colleges from a searchable dropdown and view a side-by-side comparison table. The table must compare: fees, national ranking, global ranking, eligibility criteria, available courses, college type, location, and admission deadlines. Include a 'Best Value' highlight for the most affordable eligible option.

quotes: Enables real-time comparison based on parameters like fees, rankings, eligibility criteria, and admission requirements

acceptanceCriteria: User can search and select 2 to 4 colleges using a searchable multi-select input
Comparison table renders all specified parameters as rows with colleges as columns
Cells use visual indicators (green/red) for relative better/worse values on numeric fields
'Best Value' badge is shown on the college column with the lowest fees among eligible ones
Table is horizontally scrollable on mobile

requirements.text: Build a College Map page that displays college locations on an interactive world map using a visual map component (Leaflet.js or equivalent). Each college is shown as a pin/marker. Clicking a marker opens a popup with college name, city, fees, and ranking. Include filter controls (by country, stream) to show/hide pins.

quotes: Google Maps Integration Module: Displays geographic insights and location data for colleges

acceptanceCriteria: Interactive map renders with college location markers loaded from backend data
Clicking a marker shows a popup with college name, location, fees, and ranking
Filter panel allows filtering markers by country and stream
Map is centered on India by default and supports zoom/pan
Map is responsive and usable on mobile devices

requirements.text: Build an Admission Guidelines page that provides structured, step-by-step admission guidance organized by destination (India / USA / UK / Canada / Australia). For each destination, show: required documents checklist, typical application process steps, key exam requirements (e.g., JEE, SAT, IELTS, TOEFL, GRE), and general timeline/deadlines. Use an accordion or tabbed layout.

quotes: Provides structured information about required documents, procedures, and application steps

acceptanceCriteria: Page contains tabs or accordion sections for each of the 5 destinations
Each section lists required documents as a checklist
Application steps are shown as a numbered sequence
Exam requirements are listed with names and score thresholds
Content is readable, well-structured, and scrollable on all screen sizes

requirements.text: Build an AI Chatbot widget that appears as a floating chat button on all pages. When opened, it renders a chat interface where users can type questions. The chatbot backend should match user queries against a predefined knowledge base (colleges, fees, courses, admission requirements, documents) using keyword/intent matching and return relevant answers. Support at least the following intents: fee inquiry, eligibility inquiry, document requirements, application deadline, course availability, and ranking inquiry.

quotes: NLP-based AI chatbot allows students to ask questions related to colleges, courses, fees, documents, and admission processes
Performs intent recognition and entity extraction to understand and respond to student queries

acceptanceCriteria: Floating chat button is visible on all pages in the bottom-right corner
Chat window opens/closes on button click and shows conversation history
Backend processes text queries and returns relevant responses from the knowledge base
At least 6 intents are handled: fees, eligibility, documents, deadlines, courses, rankings
Unrecognized queries return a helpful fallback message with suggestions
Chat history persists during the user session

requirements.text: Add a College Detail page accessible from recommendation cards and comparison results. The page must display full college information: name, description, location, all available courses, fees breakdown, eligibility criteria, required documents, application steps, ranking details, and a mini map pin showing its location.
acceptanceCriteria: College detail page is accessible via a unique route (e.g., /college/:id)
All stored college fields are displayed in a structured layout with sections
A mini map shows the college's geographic location
Page includes a 'Add to Comparison' button
Breadcrumb navigation allows returning to Recommendations or Comparison

requirements.text: Add a responsive top navigation bar with the EduMap logo, links to Home, Profile, Recommendations, Compare, Map, Guidelines, and a Chatbot toggle. Include a mobile hamburger menu for small screens.
acceptanceCriteria: Navigation bar is present on all pages
All six page links navigate to correct routes
Active route is visually highlighted in the nav
Mobile hamburger menu collapses/expands on toggle
EduMap logo is displayed on the left side of the nav

constraints: Do not integrate real Google Maps API — use an open-source map library (e.g., Leaflet with OpenStreetMap tiles) for the map module
Do not integrate real ML/NLP external services — implement scoring and intent matching logic entirely within the Motoko backend
Do not integrate external authentication — user profiles are stored by session/key without login for now
All college data must be seeded in the backend; no external database connections
nonGoals: Real-time ML model training or external ML API integration
User account login or Internet Identity authentication
Payment processing or application fee collection
Admin dashboard for managing college data

required: EduMap logo mark — a globe with a graduation cap and map pin overlay in teal and amber tones (edumap-logo.dim_256x256.png)
Hero section background illustration — abstract world map with glowing connection lines and university building silhouettes in deep teal gradient (hero-bg.dim_1440x600.png)
Graduation student illustration — diverse students celebrating with documents and a globe, flat illustration style in teal and amber (students-hero.dim_600x400.png)
